Further research is needed to confirm its classification. Rorida droserifolia is closely related to other sundew species, which are known for their unique ability to capture and digest insects.

The health benefits of Drosera include its use as an expectorant, to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and as an anti-inflammatory to reduce swelling and pain.

The bioactive constituents of Drosera include iridoids, such as droserone and droserol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as flavonoids and terpenoids.
